data.title: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest adds Alibaba and Baidu, lowers holdings in AMD
Stock index futures tick up ahead of a slew of macroeconomic releases, Fed speak
data.description:
Stock index futures traded higher on Thursday, as investors awaited a slew of macroeconomic releases and Fed speak slated for the day.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.14%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.61%.

Wall Street's major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, marking a second day of declines, as struggling U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

“Risk assets have struggled to find their footing over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 posting another modest decline after the AI-driven selloff on Tuesday. Overnight futures have recouped around half those losses, but it does seem that we've paused for breath in recent days. There hasn’t been a major catalyst driving this, but decent housing data and stronger oil prices yesterday led to some doubts about the prospect of rapid rate cuts from the Federal Reserve,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

There is a barrage of economic releases on Thursday. 

The August durable goods orders will come in before the opening bell. Total durable goods orders are seen showing a decrease of 0.5% on the month.

GDP will land at the same time. The final revision for Q2 growth is expected to show no change from 3.3% in the last report.

“The U.S. releases revised second-quarter GDP data. The market consensus is for no change. U.S. growth has been distorted this year—export and inventory data swung wildly between the first and second quarters. Unfortunately, the fiction of annualization assumes that what happens in a quarter, however unique, is continually repeated. This exaggerates first-quarter weakness and second-quarter strength,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The initial jobless claims and international trade in goods (advance) will also come in at the same time. Claims are seen at 238K in the latest week and the consensus sees the goods trade gap at $94.4 billion in August versus $103.9 billion in July.

“U.S. initial and continuing jobless claims data will get attention, as the Fed prioritizes weak labor markets over higher inflation,” Donovan said. 

During market hours, the August existing home sales report will land. Sales are expected to stay soft at a 3.95 million rate in August versus 4.01 million in July.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day. 

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.

data.title: Nippon Steel notes 'minor difference' with Trump admin over golden share in U.S. Steel deal
data.description:
• A minor disagreement remains between Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) and the U.S. government over the authority of a golden share linked to its planned acquisition of U.S. Steel, the Japanese steelmaker’s president said on Thursday.
  • Last week, the Wall Street Journal reported that U.S. President Donald Trump's administration had blocked U.S. Steel's plan to shut down production at one of its plants in Illinois, flexing its so-called golden share authority.
  • "There is minor difference in views regarding the national security agreements and the authority of the golden share," Nippon Steel President Tadashi Imai told reporters, as per Reuters.
  • He did not elaborate but said the recent U.S. move reflected the U.S. government's policy of protecting domestic production bases and jobs across various sectors, the report said.
  • Japan's top steelmaker closed its $14.9 billion acquisition of U.S. Steel in June, agreeing to give Washington unusual power to help end its 18-month battle to reach a deal.

data.title: Nokia and Nscale team up to accelerate AI data center deployments
Birkenstock raises 2025 revenue outlook to €2.09B, buys German factory for €18M
data.description:
Birkenstock (NYSE:BIRK) on Thursday said that it expects its fiscal 2025 revenues to total at least €2.09 billion, ahead of its prior guidance, and announced the acquisition of a production facility near Dresden, Germany, for a net price of €18 million.

Birkenstock (NYSE:BIRK) also pre-announced its fiscal fourth quarter revenues, which it expects to total at least €520 million, growth of at least 14% on a reported basis and 18% in constant currency.

This would result in full-year revenue of at least €2.09 billion, growth of 15.9% on a reported basis and 17.5% in constant currency, above guidance of 15-17%.

The German sandal maker also reaffirmed its target for adjusted core earnings in the range of 31.3-31.8% for the full fiscal year 2025, despite headwinds from F/X.  

On the acquisition, Birkenstock (BIRK), whose U.S.-listed stock has declined 20% year-to-date, said it expects the deal to close in the fiscal first quarter.

"The opportunity to acquire this property for the attractive price of €240 per square meter arose due to the bankruptcy of its current owner. The acquisition will fast track the company's manufacturing capacity buildup plans at a favorable cost to a new build," it said.

Birkenstock will continue to look for opportunities to acquire assets in the European Union, it added.

data.title: Encore Capital prices upsized $500M senior notes offering
Apple criticizes EU tech law for delays in adding new features
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) blamed the European Union’s Digital Markets Act (DMA) for delaying the rollout of several new features to users in the region, arguing that the law’s requirements create privacy and security risks and force delays in bringing new technologies to market.

The iPhone maker said the regulation has led to a delay in the release of a number of products in the EU, such as iPhone Mirroring to Mac and Live Translation with AirPods.

"Over time, it’s become clear that the DMA isn’t helping markets. It’s making it harder to do business in Europe." Apple said in a statement.

"Despite our concerns with the DMA, teams across Apple are spending thousands of hours to bring new features to the European Union while meeting the law’s requirements. But it’s become clear that we can’t solve every problem the DMA creates." it added.

The smartphone manufacturer claimed that the European Commission rejected its suggested measures and that it has not been able to meet its criteria without jeopardizing user data.

Previously, to comply with the bloc's antitrust order, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) modified the regulations and fees in its App Store in the EU in June.

data.title: Emmis Acquisition prices 10M unit IPO at $10 per unit
data.description:
• Blank check company Emmis Acquisition (NASDAQ:EMIS) on Wednesday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 10,000,000 units for $10.00 per unit.
  • The units are expected to be listed on Nasdaq and begin trading tomorrow, September 25, under the ticker symbol “EMISU.”
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one right to receive one tenth (1/10) of one Class A ordinary share upon the consummation of an initial business combination.
  • An amount equal to $10.00 per unit will be deposited into a trust account upon the closing of the offering.

data.title: Lilly plans to launch experimental weight-loss pill in India - report
data.description:
• Pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) aims to bring its experimental oral weight-loss drug orforpligron to India as it sees scope for such products in the world's most populous nation, a senior executive told Reuters on Thursday.
  • "I do think there is promise for products like that (orforpligron) in India, if it does get approved," Winselow Tucker, Lilly India's president, said at an event in Mumbai.
  • Tucker, however, did not indicate when Lilly plans to launch the drug.
  • The drugmaker has bid for approval for the experimental pill after the latest clinical data showed it lowered average blood sugar and weight in a head-to-head trial of adults with type 2 diabetes more effectively than rival Novo Nordisk's (NVO) older GLP-1 pill Rybelsus.

data.title: MBX Biosciences stock down on pricing upsized public offering at $18.00 per share
data.description:
MBX Biosciences (NASDAQ:MBX) has priced its public offering of 11,108,055 shares at $18.00 per share. The upsized offering is expected to generate gross proceeds of approximately $199.9 million for the company.

The company had initially planned to offer 10 million shares.

Additionally, MBX has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,666,208 shares.

The offering is expected to close on September 26, 2025.

MBX stock were down 2.2% after-hours on Wednesday.
